description: Event-driven, change-triggered single-window capture that emits timestamped screenshots + metadata for AI agents; plus a `record` mode that bundles a narrated screen recording + timestamped transcript into an LLM package.

binary.path_note: Paths are relative to the repository root. Resolve relative_path against your checkout to get an absolute path.

commands.name: windows
commands.stdout: table with columns: HWND, EXE, TITLE

commands.name: watch
commands.summary: Capture a window into a session directory until stopped.
commands.stop: Ctrl+C, or the target window closes

selectors.--title: case-insensitive substring matched against the window title (literal, not a regex)
selectors.--pid: exact: the window owned by this process id (avoids stale-window matches on repeat captures of the same exe)

options.--roi: crop capture + detection + output to a pixel region X,Y,W,H (clips host chrome); window.rect still reports the full source window
options.--config: path to a framewatch.toml base config (flags override it)

lifecycle_options.--wait: poll/retry up to N seconds for the target window to appear (no launch-order race)
lifecycle_options.--until-settled: exit after the first settled frame (deterministic one-shot)
lifecycle_options.--duration: exit after N seconds (time-bounded; also stops an idle window)
lifecycle_options.--frames: exit after N images have been saved

commands.name: shot
commands.summary: One settled frame to a single chosen file; optionally launch+capture+kill a program. Best for scripted/batch capture.

selectors.--launch: spawn this program and capture its window by PID, then kill it on exit (whitespace-split; "..." groups an arg)
selectors.--pid: capture the window owned by this process id (exact)
selectors.--title: case-insensitive substring matched against the window title (literal, not a regex)

options.--out-file: exact PNG path to write (required, deterministic)
options.--timeout: overall budget seconds to wait for window + settle (default 20)
options.--settle-best-effort: if nothing settles before timeout, write the latest frame anyway

commands.contract: On success prints the written path to stdout and exits 0. If no frame settles before --timeout (and --settle-best-effort is unset), writes nothing and exits non-zero (3).

commands.name: record
commands.summary: Continuously record a window to video while narrating into the mic, transcribe locally, and write an LLM package (video + timestamped transcript + prompt). Requires the `record` feature and ffmpeg on PATH.
commands.stop: Ctrl+C (mp4 is finalized cleanly), or after --duration

selectors.--title: case-insensitive substring matched against the window title (literal, not a regex)
selectors.--pid: exact: the window owned by this process id
selectors.--launch: spawn this program and record its window by PID, then kill it on stop

options.--out: output parent directory (a per-recording subdir is created)
options.--duration: auto-stop after N seconds (otherwise record until Ctrl+C)
options.--fps: target video frames per second (default 30, clamped 1..=60)
options.--mic: microphone device name substring (default: system default input)
options.--no-audio: record video only (no microphone / transcription); also the automatic fallback when no input device is available
options.--roi: crop the recorded region to a pixel rect X,Y,W,H
options.--wait: wait up to N seconds for the target window to appear
options.--transcribe-cmd: transcribe by shelling out to a local transcriber; {audio}/{output} substituted; command emits framewatch JSON or SRT

commands.stdout: first line: the package directory; last line: the PROMPT.md path

commands.name: gui
commands.summary: Launch the GUI window picker / ROI editor (for humans).

output.timeline_format: JSON Lines (one event object per line, chronological)

timeline_event_fields.image: string path relative to session dir, or null
timeline_event_fields.window: { title, exe, class, hwnd, rect:[x,y,w,h], dpi, foreground }
timeline_event_fields.change: { changed_tiles, tile_grid:[cols,rows], area_ratio, bboxes:[[x,y,w,h]], dhash?, hamming_to_prev_emit? }
timeline_event_fields.coalesced_frames: u32 frames collapsed since previous saved image

consume_algorithm: Read session.json for target app, time range, config, and ROI hints.
Stream timeline.jsonl line by line (each line is a complete JSON object).
Open images only for events with kind == 'settled' or kind == 'busy_end'.
Use coalesced_frames to know how much activity each saved image represents.
Use busy_start / value_sample notes for timing context without opening images.

recording_output.note: Produced by the `record` command (see docs section 6 + dist/sample-recording).
recording_output.transcript_json_shape: { language?, duration_ms, segments: [{ start_ms, end_ms, text }] }
recording_output.timestamp_meaning: segment start_ms/end_ms are ms from the start of recording.mp4 — seek the video to a quoted instruction, e.g. ffmpeg -ss <start_ms/1000> -i recording.mp4 -frames:v 1 frame.png
consume_algorithm: Read PROMPT.md — it is self-contained (transcript inline) for text-only models.
If you can ingest video, open recording.mp4 and use each segment's start_ms to locate the on-screen moment an instruction refers to.
recording.json records the engine used and video/audio metadata.

library.cargo_dependency: framewatch = { path = "../framewatch", default-features = false }  # or version = "0.1" once published

platform_notes: Live capture (windows/watch commands and default_backend) requires Windows with the wgc feature. The pure detection engine and sinks compile and run on all platforms.
